summary: Shelfsort P3 final batch — frontend verification PASS (4/4). (1) BookReadingInsights pill strip mounts on /book/bk_tester_0 with [data-testid='book-reading-insights'] container and [data-testid='reread-pill'] yellow 'Re-read' badge (text='Re-read', title='3 backward jumps in the last 90 days', Repeat SVG icon present) after seeding 10 cursor_history rows with the documented [0.1, 0.3, 0.55, 0.8, 1.0, 0.05, 1.0, 0.1, 1.0, 0.2] percent trace. Backend /api/books/bk_tester_0/reread-signal returned {is_reread:true, backward_jumps:3, peak_percent:1.0}. Pace and cohort pills did not render (acceptable per request — no baseline books / below 5-user cohort threshold). (2) BookReadingInsights hides gracefully on /book/bk_tester_2 with no cursor_history: [data-testid='book-detail-title'] still mounts (page does NOT crash) and [data-testid='book-reading-insights'] is absent from the DOM (showAny short-circuit returns null). (3) SSE-driven Resume badge refresh on /library/all works end-to-end: with localStorage.shelfsort-device-id='test-tab-A-device' and a POST /api/books/bk_tester_0/cursor (device_id='phone-OTHER', percent=0.3, device_label='iPhone OTHER'), the [data-testid='cross-device-hint-bk_tester_0'] yellow Resume badge appeared within 500ms (text='Resume', title='Picked up on iPhone OTHER') without a page refresh, confirming AllBooksPage's useEventStream subscription to 'reading_cursor' triggers fetchHints. (4) Phase-6 chapter-helper extraction shim — /api/books/bk_tester_0/diff returns HTTP 400 with the structured payload {"detail":"No counterpart version found. Refresh this book first to create a version history, or pass ?vs={other_book_id}."}. Status 400 (not 500) proves the diff_chapters re-export from utils/epub_chapters lands cleanly and the route reaches its business-logic guard — no ImportError. Reading-insights endpoints /reread-signal, /pace-percentile, /aggregate-cursor all returned HTTP 200 with proper schemas.

verification_passes.reread_pill_renders_on_bk_tester_0_after: PASS — [data-testid='book-reading-insights'] + [data-testid='reread-pill'] present; text='Re-read', title='3 backward jumps in the last 90 days', icon SVG present
verification_passes.book_reading_insights_hides_on_bk_tester: PASS — book-detail-title mounts, book-reading-insights absent, no crash
verification_passes.sse_resume_badge_refresh: PASS — Resume badge appeared 500ms after POST /api/books/bk_tester_0/cursor with device_id='phone-OTHER'; text='Resume', title='Picked up on iPhone OTHER'
verification_passes.phase6_diff_route_shim: PASS — /api/books/bk_tester_0/diff => HTTP 400 with structured 'No counterpart version found' detail (no ImportError, diff_chapters re-export from utils/epub_chapters reachable)

critical_code_review_comments: Minor: BookReadingInsights useEffect makes 3 parallel API calls on every BookDetail mount even when the book has zero cursor_history — endpoints already short-circuit cheaply (returning have_data:false) but on a library with hundreds of detail-page visits per session, batching these into a single /api/books/{id}/insights endpoint that returns all three signals at once would cut frontend network chatter by 67%.
Cohort threshold (5 users) for aggregate-cursor is hardcoded; consider surfacing it via /api/config so the same threshold can be tuned without a frontend redeploy.

Direct goto('/book/<id>') from a fresh tab still races with AuthContext (carryover from iter24): the working pattern is to land on /library via login, THEN history.pushState. AuthContext gating on the '/' route would solve this preview-deploy testing quirk.

success_rate.backend: 100% (4/4 endpoints verified: reread-signal, pace-percentile, aggregate-cursor, diff)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (3/3 UI flows: pill strip mount, graceful hide, SSE Resume badge refresh)

test_credentials: shelfsort-tester@example.com / tester123! (user_a18dde45958d) — used as documented; no admin elevation needed for this iter.
seed_data_creation: Inserted 10 cursor_history rows for user_a18dde45958d/bk_tester_0 with percent trace [0.1, 0.3, 0.55, 0.8, 1.0, 0.05, 1.0, 0.1, 1.0, 0.2] and ascending ts ISO strings spanning the last 10 minutes (TEST_seed:true marker). Also inserted 1 reading_cursors row for bk_tester_0 with device_id='phone-OTHER' via the POST /api/books/bk_tester_0/cursor call to exercise the SSE flow. Both cleaned up at end of run: cursor_history deletedCount=10, reading_cursors deletedCount=1.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All three P3 final-batch frontend features (BookReadingInsights pill strip, SSE Resume badge refresh, Phase-6 diff shim) are landed and verified. Field name gotcha: cursor_history rows are queried by the route on field 'ts' (ISO string) not 'recorded_at' — first seed attempt with recorded_at returned is_reread:false; reseed with ts as ISO string returned is_reread:true. To re-verify in the future: seed 10 rows with the documented percent trace, ts as ISO strings ascending within the 90-day window, then GET /api/books/<id>/reread-signal. For SSE: login in tab A, set localStorage.shelfsort-device-id to anything other than 'phone-OTHER', then POST /api/books/<id>/cursor with device_id='phone-OTHER' and watch the cross-device-hint testid appear within 1s. Direct goto deep-links still race AuthContext; use login-then-pushState pattern.
